If it's for you, I...
Closure! Over here!
Huh?
Time to move, Doctor. The zipline's ready. Give me your hand!
A burst of light explodes as the sword pierces the ground.
Time stops in an instant.
...Confessarius witchcraft.
General!
Dame Kal'tsit...
She approaches the end of this cycle.
Rhodes Island's Cautus was rescued. The Sanguinarch of Vampires is pursuing the Banshee and Ascalon.
What of Theresa?
I just received Her Highness's signal, general.
It's ready.
Good.
The Regent looks up at the overcast sky and the walls in the distance.
The true battlefield of the Sarkaz lies beyond the walls.
It's time to begin.
Are you ready?
There's nothing to make ready.
Right, we're finally here.
Vina, I must—
We must bear witness to all this.
An elaborate black structure lies at the end of the tunnel.
Generation after generation, the greatest minds in Victoria came together to build it.
All this, just to hold a sword.
If few are the heroes who can use it to conquer Catastrophe, then let humanity use its wisdom to raise a shield against the storm.
These structures, the entire building, was made for this.
The voices rise up again.
The calls are no longer urgent, no longer sharp, no longer attempting to persuade or incite.
She feels a tired sense of peace.
She has been here before. She has been guided to this place, long before her memories took shape.
Just like then, she knows where she must go, what she must take.
It is right there, in the middle of the room, unguarded, so unremarkable it might as well have been a tiny decoration in this grand building.
Kyngasycath, the Sighs of Kings.
It lay there and continues to do so, over the centuries, through countless reforgings.
Siege extends a hand towards it.
Broken shadows fill the space, rising and ebbing, resonating.
Alexandrina. Alexandrina Vina Victoria. They whisper her name.
She touches the sword.
Shards of days gone by pass through her body. She hears a boom in her mind.
Suddenly, she understands.
She is the source of all those visions and sounds.
They are her doubts, confusion, regrets, recollections.
Things she has forgotten, and things she intentionally forgot.
A great hand raises her. Londinium stands up from beneath her, taking her across the land of Victoria.
The land that she and her friends walked.
Countless voices superimpose upon one another.
Victoria, Victoria.
This is Victoria.
Her Victoria.

lines.st: 'Vina, Vina.'

lines.x: The voices call out.
Siege opens her eyes. There are no illusions now, no tides. She holds in her hand the Sighs of Kings, symbol of the Victorian monarchy.
There is little to show that it is a sword of legend. It is unremarkable in size and design.
It looks like an ordinary sword.
You have the Sighs of Kings, Vina.

It's... lighter than I expected.
Let's go. Time to go home.
We—
Siege wants to turn around and feel the sword's warmth with her friends.
Don't.
But she feels a colder blade in her back.
...Allerdale.
Please! Don't. Don't turn around.
Give me the sword.
Your Highness Alexandrina... I'm sorry.
I have to...
I have to do this.
Allerdale's voice is calm, but filled with a terrible, resolute sorrow.
Siege recalls the 'memoirs' that Morgan wrote in jest.
A handful of pages, clumsily written, with less-than-elegant vocabulary.
The story starts with a fictional fight. Everyone returns to base after the fight. Allerdale makes a cream stew for them in the story.
She laughs in her heart. Allerdale made a stew, yes, but what the memoirs omitted is that...
It did not exactly taste great.
Siege!
Oh—!
Don't move.
You... you too...
I don't want to hurt you.
But I have a debt to pay.
Our mission is to help Allerdale remove the sword from the Mausoleum of Kings.
Finish this job, and I can retire.
It was an offer I couldn't refuse.
We just mourned the heroes who fell victim to betrayal together.
I thought you would understand.
But in the end, you're no different from them.
I'm ashamed I thought about trusting you, even for a moment.
Like I said, living is hard.
I'm sorry. I want all of us to keep on living. I'll try to make sure we do just that.
We've both been pushed out of death's way by our friends.
Unfortunately, our paths happen to diverge this time.
Allerdale...
Your Highness.
Please hand over the Sighs of Kings, for your own safety.
Is this the only way?
I betrayed your trust. There's nothing I can say about that.
You can admonish me, reproach me, try to defeat me. You have the right to do so.
But I will leave with the sword.
This is my... destiny... in Londinium.
You said the Sighs of Kings would protect Londinium from the storm.
I don't want to drag Londinium into the abyss either. I won't let her fall into Sarkaz hands.
Into the hands of the dukes, then.
Whoever is behind you wants to use the Sighs of Kings to intimidate the other dukes and subjugate them.
They don't care about protecting Victoria.
We may be meek, but we're in the middle of all this.
With the people who have been hurt and abused.
I know.
I know what she's after, but it doesn't concern me.
I'm only doing... I only care about what I can actually influence.
What about the Self-Salvation Corps?
You built it with Clovisia. You protected it with your honor.
I have no honor.
Then you protected it with your life.
What do I tell the Corps fighters, if you're really going to leave?
Clovisia will handle it.
I no longer have the strength to care about what they think of me.
You claim you only care about what you can influence.
Aren't you underestimating what you already have?
What does one dare to have, when you know it's only a matter of time before you lose it?
What does one dare to reveal, when what everyone trusts and respects is just an illusion?
What can one hold on to, when you realize this is the one and only end of your path?
This isn't even the outcome of a choice.
I simply ended up here. Fate has pushed me to this place, before I even realized it.
We could never have walked together.
We met when we were little, didn't we?
At Cumberland Manor.
I thought you had forgotten.
I almost had... I was with Gawain that day.
The creature that was like the sun told us we would meet again, someday.
But he could not have guessed that it would end this way.
You won't give me the sword, Vina.
Then swing it. We must bear witness...
Allerdale.
Remember my promise?

Enough! Say no more.
I carry nothing on my shoulders. I'll just be your brain trust.
Sorry... I forgot you had Morgan.
I'll just be a thug then.
We'll bomb the Sarkaz camp together. You light a fire, and I'll kick their commander's arse once there's smoke.
I'm joking.
I know.
My path is long determined, Vina.
Why do you say that? You're still with us. The Glasgow Gang has more stories to write.
So...
The answer is yes.
I'll make sure you come back unharmed after kicking that commander's arse.
That's my promise.
It's not a joke. Never has been, never will be.
We'll all make it out of this in one piece.
A sound breaks the stalemate.
Was it an illusion?
Siege looks around. The massive statues remain still in the shadows. None of the dead Sarkaz come back to life.
Again.
Nearby, right in front of her.
The sound of steam gushing, increasingly rapid.
Increasingly loud, increasingly clear, until it fills the entire space.
W-What's that?
Allerdale's heart skips a beat.
She can feel it.
The premonition of fate echoes again cruelly.
In front of you, Siege!
Is that...?
A suit of steam armor.
The spurts of white steam stand out in front of the dark hue of the armor.
Spurt after spurt.
Like breathing.
The fog parts before him. He awakens from history.
Nothing can stop him. Nothing can defeat him.
Not abandonment, not betrayal.
Not even death.
It's moving! Look, it's moving!
Man alive, what is happening here?!
Agh! Bolts do nothing!
Get the grenades!
The massive suit of armor rises from the rubble.
Heavy footsteps.
Heavy gushing.
It walks toward the panicked crowd, one step at a time.
(Heavy gushing)
There's no point.
You're from Sargon, Totter. You wouldn't understand.
They were the stuff of our bedtime stories.
It's still stories of the Steam Knights I tell when I put my kids to bed.
How are we supposed to defeat him?
How do we defeat our pride, our glory, our hero, who's already been betrayed once?
We can't defeat him.
This is a... a Victorian Steam Knight.
Fall back! Fall back!
What's that sound?
Sounds like steam.
How?
Definitely steam. They're back!
I know I backed the right one! This is where Vina hid her joker!
Morgan, we've got the Steam Knights on our side!
There are Steam Knights left in Londinium? Here?
Are they guarding the Mausoleum of Kings? The Sighs of Kings?
Ha, who cares what they're doing?
Vina is their king!
When we come out up top with the Steam Knights, those Sarkaz will... hey, Morgan, what's that look on your face for?
Vina's gonna get the Sighs of Kings.
Four years. The Steam Knights have stayed by the sword for four years, in this sunless dungeon.
They didn't show up when the Sarkaz occupied the city. They didn't show up when the Sarkaz took over the factories. They didn't show up when the Sarkaz burned the noble houses.
What do they think?
Are they gonna side with intruders into the Mausoleum of Kings?
Who's there?!
Take your weapon, Morgan.
They're strong... they're all around us.
Hah, this is getting interesting.
Why are the hoodlums of Norport here?
The Glasgow Gang? Under Alexandrina?
Show your face, now!
Hmph. There are enough variables without that sound of gushing steam rising in Londinium again.
What would the Duke think if she knew there were survivors among the Steam Knights?
Would she be uneasy? Or glad that the glorious heroes of old have returned?
Ha, glory...
Let's take care of the hoodlums. Don't let them get in Allerdale's way.
(Heavy gushing)
...Steam Knights.
Four years... there are survivors among the Steam Knights?
Those marks... they should have been lethal wounds. The Sarkaz spikes destroyed the cores. There's no way the wearer would survive.
Plus, no one could enter or leave the Mausoleum of Kings without the royal key.
He's spent four years in this dark dungeon...
Even if he survived the initial carnage, how could he have survived in this place?
What does the man in the armor look like now?
(Heavy gushing)
Who are... you...?
No... what are you?
A flame sparks in the helmet of the armor.
Allerdale can feel a cold gaze upon herself.
Upon everyone here.
The flame...
The armor roaring in the flames.
It was a dream from her childhood.
Her hope for salvation.
The destruction that she witnessed with her own eyes.
A nightmare, too terrible to gaze upon.
He is back. Back before her.
Allerdale cannot keep herself from trembling.
Have you come to settle this?
(Heavy gushing)
Allerdale!
Look out!
Steam Knight, I am Alexandrina Vina Victoria, heir to the Aslan kings.
Halt your attack, knight.
We are not your enemies.

lines.st: 'Victoria.'

lines.x: Siege hears a voice echo above her head.
Was it the Steam Knight? She is unsure. It sounded more like the rumbling of a machine than a human voice.
The spurts of white steam become more rapid and forceful.
The dark knight raises his massive weapon towards her.
'Victoria.'
Siege suddenly understands. The Steam Knight is not calling her name.
It is looking at her sword.
The Sighs of Kings, the symbol of the royal house of Victoria.
'Victoria.'
He called the sword Victoria.
The Victoria that he swore to defend unto his last breath.
Glorious Victoria betrayed him. Now, he will defend the symbol of Victoria.
The last knight of the empire shall take revenge on all those who transgress against her.
He charges towards the heir to the throne.
Get out of the way, Siege!
Why did the Steam Knight attack you?
Can't he tell...?
Get down!
Argh... *cough*...
The sword of fire...
Don't let it hit you!
It's gone mad! We can't stop it!
Get out of here! Get out! Get out...
Gah!
We'll all be dead before long.
Get your men out.
Do as Vina says.
Mission aborted, then.
Looks like my retirement plan will have to wait.
Dagda, take the mercs and get out.
Your Highness!
You're the last one who bowed to them.
Take their story back, let it see the light of day.
The story belongs to all of us, Siege.
We should tell it together.
I don't know what's come over you, Allerdale.
I used to respect you. I thought you were worthy of a knight's honor. No, you were a knight paragon in my mind.
Always at the forefront, not caring for fame and fortune, not afraid of any foe.
You were Cumberland the Ever on High!
Nothing can remain on High forever, Dagda.
But we fought together, didn't we?
Your story is here too, Allerdale.
If you want to come back.
I can't do that, can I?
It depends on you.
But first, we deal with this knight.
My master taught me that a duel between knights must be fair and honorable.
Our foe is a glorious hero.
Allow me to stand by your side, Your Highness.
...Of course.
Fight... him?
A pathetic, despicable, lying traitor.
How can one like that challenge fate?
It's a wonderful fable, isn't it?
An innocent child wanted to become a hero. But she went bad, and when the hero returned from history, she found his sword at her throat.
The story should end with her death.
She should lose everything. Never to fulfill her destiny.
Vina, I...
I don't want to hear you talk about 'destiny' anymore, Allerdale.
Hold your head high, Allerdale Cumberland. There is no such thing as destiny, for me or for you.
I need to calm him down. Then, if you still want this sword, all you need to do is challenge me, and I'll abide.
This is not your bloody destiny, Allerdale. What you want to do, do it.
But don't say that word anymore.
(Heavy gushing)
He's coming.
The sound of steam.
Heavy gushing.

lines.st: 'Stand up, **.'
'Training time, **.'
'Not bad, **. A Steam Knight at your age! You're our pride, and the pride of Victoria!'

lines.x: Unintelligible syllables. Is someone calling his name?
But he cannot remember his own name anymore.
Evans? Jones? Wilson? Lynch?
None of them, and all of them.

lines.st: 'We are the Steam Knights.'
'We vow to defend Victoria unto our last breath.'

lines.x: But why...
Why were his fellows hidden in the shadows behind the outlanders' swords?
His comrades fell, one by one.
In the end, he was the only one left. He was The Last Steam Knight.
He cannot fall here. The Steam Knights must not fall.
Victoria needs him. He must fulfill his duty.
No medicine, no matter. The armor will repair his flesh and blood.
No food, no matter. There are always critters crawling in the darkness.
No light, no matter. The sword gives him all the light he needs.
He is not dead. The Steam Knights are not dead. They continue to defend Victoria.
But...
What is Victoria?
I swore an oath before the King, before the people, before the world.
I stood vigil, night after night, but where lies the light of Victoria that I have sworn fealty to?
Is Victoria its king?
They hanged the king.
Is Victoria Parliament and the nobles?
No maintenance, no supplies, no new members. They sent the Steam Knights into mortal danger time after time, in order to feed their greed.
Is Victoria the people?

The people praise the Steam Knights, honor the Steam Knights, glorify the Steam Knights. They place all their hopes, all their dreams upon the Steam Knights. Nothing can carry hopes that are so contradictory, so complex.
What is Victoria?
Does Victoria only exist in the imagination?
There is still the sword, the Sighs of Kings.
It is the symbol of Victoria, beyond all doubt.
Then it will be his Victoria.
Now, someone wants to defile his Victoria.
This is unacceptable.
He cannot lose his last—
(Heavy gushing)
'Victoria.'
D-Do you hear, knight?
I cannot command you.
I ask you to allow us to take the sword.
Not because of who I am.
Londinium needs it.
No, not Londinium.
The children shivering in orphanages need it. The farmers who lost hearth and home to war need it.
The workers pouring sweat and blood into their machines need it. The citizens who drink away their helplessness need it.
The soldiers who step forward to protect their homes need it. Rhodes Island, trying to avert disaster, needs it.
It is not a storm that it will dispel. It is the fear.
This is how it must be used.
It's not your Victoria, knight.
Allow me to pass.
Not Victoria?
Then where is Victoria?
The Steam Knight is filled with confusion and anger.
Where is Victoria?!
What is the Victoria that he served?!
(Heavy gushing)
He's getting faster!
We can't stop him!
Allerdale!
Your Highness Alexandrina.
Do you remember the day we met?
We should have spoken more. You appeared at the ducal manor, with that sword.
It was a beautiful afternoon.
There was music in the garden. The flowers bloomed brightly. People danced and talked about tomorrow. Everything was filled with hope.
I boasted that I would become a Steam Knight.
The light shone upon the sword... and upon you.
They said what happened that afternoon was a miracle.
She was running.
The freshly-trimmed grass made her feet itch. Jim the Gardener laughed by the bushes. Ailshie ran after her, calling for her to slow down.
But she was eager to run towards the freshly-blooming flowers. They were golden, shining under the sun, like the emblem of House Cumberland.
Her father picked her up, sat her on his shoulder. She lifted her tiny palm, and let her touch the lines on the steam armor.
The broken armor stands up.
Her father is gone. She has grown up, and she no longer stands before the armor. She feels the heavy weight of the armor on her shoulder.
Through her visor, she sees a golden lion at the end of the path.

lines.st: 'Allerdale Cumberland.
You will meet Vina again, someday.'

lines.x: It's been hard for me to believe in miracles, after all these years.
Ailshie told me that I don't know who I will be.
I'm afraid there won't be a 'will be'.
How old was I? I don't remember. I promised Ailshie that I'd become a Steam Knight and protect everyone.
At least, I have the chance to face a Steam Knight now.
I hear you, Vina. There's no such thing as fate or destiny.
Not for House Cumberland, not for the dukes.
To hell with destiny. I'm tired.
Tired of playing a noble, playing a leader, playing a friend.
You're the leader of the Self-Salvation Corps because they trust you.
You're my friend because I say you are.
Hah. That's enough, Vina.
Let's put an end to the fable.
Tell Morgan to cast me in a better light in her memoir, Dagda.
Use more refined words, at least.
What's going on?
The wind...
A gust of wind blows them outside.
One solemn face falls after another, as the statues of the kings collapse, one by one.
The rubble separates them from the Steam Knight... and someone else.
Siege immediately reacts.
Allerdale!
Forgive me...
Her lips move slightly.
Siege knows Allerdale called to her.
Was it 'Vina'? Or 'Your Highness'?
Siege does not know.
She may never know.
The sound is drowned by the rubble.
The door is shut.
Alright, come now, my nightmare...
My hope.
(Heavy gushing)
One scene floats to the surface of the Steam Knight's tumultuous memories.
A girl sheds tears for him before he stands up.
Pah—
Son of a—
There's nowhere to run!
Last smoke grenade. We still have a chance.
You've done enough, mercenary.
Surrender, and they might let you live.
No way. How many more years would I have to work?
Enough.
End this.
Were you the ones manipulating Allerdale?
No, it's not you. The cowardly dukes wouldn't dare show their faces here.
Allerdale has failed. Alexandrina has the Sighs of Kings.
What a boring conclusion.
You know what?
You will do.
You will pay for all that you've done!

lines.st:
Act 4
——————
Finale

lines.x: Stop, traitor!
Nothing can stop me. You must pay for your crimes.
You will die with me!
The death of the flesh does not daunt me. Countless eyes will bear witness.
Brave children, you will carry the torch.
You will remember my virtues, and take this hard-earned peace into tomorrow.
The end.
That was wonderful, Lady Golding. A great success.
The product of a year of everyone's hard work.
I wish we were in more peaceful times. That way, Heidi could have been here to see it.
It's times like these that we need a play like this.
Look how happy the kids are—
Hey, Leo! Don't run with the props!
Get back here!
Get back here!
Run, run! I'll come for you! Great Victoria will come for you!
Hie over the valleys and over the rivers, rumble-ye-rumble...
Crush them! Squeeze the blood from them!
Die! You'll all die! I'll catch you and kill you!
Squeeze it out!
Surrender, Sarkaz!
Surrender!
Or not, because I'll catch you!
Stop! You're ruining Lady Golding's handmade 'steam armor'!
They...
Don't be angry, Lady Golding. They're just playing a game.
A game...?
Yes. The play has ended.
'Squeeze the blood from them'?
It's... the Sarkaz...
She sees before her eyes the red that filled the bookstore.
She does not even have the strength to vomit.
It's normal.
We're in a war, after all.
The Sarkaz have been arresting people left and right, often in violent ways. The children see them, and emulate their behavior.
Do you know, Molly...?
Many remnants of Gaul harbor fear of the Steam Knights.
Those who were part of the war will never forget the sound of steam gushing outside their window that night.
But I had hoped that the Steam Knights would be more than just a weapon of death to the children.
I had hoped that there would be beautiful hope in their hearts when they play the Steam Knight.
I thought this was a war I could win.
Golding's gaze follows the children.
The foam-made steam armor falls to the ground. She tries to pick it up, but pulls back sharply, as though she's been burned.
I thought I was teaching the children...
Now, I realize that they were the ones teaching me a lesson.
This, here, is the ending. The true ending.
Before the wheels of time...
What we do may be utterly meaningless.
The lessons of the books are distant, blurred.
But no one can avoid being shaped by the times.
If they belong to blood, violence and war...
Then, our children will belong to blood, violence and war.
This is unavoidable.
What... what should I...
How could I dream of fighting it?
Vina!
Did I fall sleep?
You're lucky to be out for just a little bit after your injuries.
Siege, Self-Salvation Corps reinforcements have arrived. This place is relatively safe. Rest well; we'll be at your side.
Mm...
She scans the faces of her friends.
Everyone is bruised and battered, just as she is. But they are alive, and there is still hope in their eyes.
Except...
...Allerdale.
The door to the Mausoleum of Kings has closed once again.
The Sarkaz have noticed the sound of the Steam Knight, and they must have guessed that the Sighs of Kings has left its home. They're sending men to the palace.
Our scouts have tried to get close to the entrance, but there has been no sign of Allerdale.
Do you want to talk about what happened below?
Just as the warriors imagined.
Allerdale Cumberland sacrificed herself to save us.
Sounds like something Allerdale would do. Cumberland the Ever on High.
Cumberland...
There's no such thing as Cumberland the Ever on High, Clovisia.
Allerdale protected what she wanted to protect with her life.
Not out of any duty... simply of her own accord.
Her own accord...
Yes, she was brave.
Shame... I had a surprise for Allerdale when she came back.
This is...
The ducal manor is in ruins, but the scouts found this among the ashes.
The steam armor of House Cumberland.
It wasn't lost in the fire...
Haha.
If only she could see this.
Then she would have known that there is no such thing as a fixed path.
The fire had not taken away all the hopes that she placed on the armor.
Clovisia, we saw a Steam Knight in the Mausoleum of Kings.
Maybe the last one.
Four years ago, the Duke's men lured the Steam Knights into the Mausoleum of Kings, where they were ambushed by the Sarkaz.
That passage is the tomb of the Steam Knights.
What a gruesome scene it must have been, even though I was not there to see it.
He attacked you, didn't he? He attacked the heir to the throne of Victoria.
He was just doing his job.
He's defending the Sighs of Kings, even if it was merely an excuse to lure the Steam Knights into the mausoleum.
He witnessed comrade after comrade fall, and spent so many years in darkness. The pain must have gnawed away at his sanity.
Words like these are too simple to describe what went through the mind of this warrior.
On one side, the tombs of the kings. On the other, the bodies of his fellows. He defended the silent resting place for countless days and nights.
He was no madman or ghost. His will was so strong that it conquered death.
By his own will, he exacted revenge on a Victoria that betrayed him.
By his own will, he defended the symbol of Victoria.
I don't know if we'll ever see him again.
If... if he can escape the darkness...
May he once again find what he wants to defend.
We found the mercenaries who went to Highbury, general.
Bring them forward.
Yes, sir.
*cough* *cough* L-Let me go!
A mere girl...
She was found tied up on the streets with her fellow mercenaries.
I checked her weapons, and there were few signs of use. She hardly even qualifies as a merc.
Is that so?
Speak, mercenary.
Did you not kill a single Victorian?
Uwah...
Are you afraid of me?
N-No!
I-I failed my mission. You can punish me. But l-let the others go!
Take her back.
What...?
General...
You said she hardly qualifies as a mercenary.
Young Sarkaz like her don't understand what we fight for, nor the true face of our enemy, because no one ever taught her.
The real war is about to begin.
I will show her what this war means to the Sarkaz. She will watch it unfold by my side.
Feist!
Rockrock... ha, Squad 11 of the Self-Salvation Corps, back together again, eh?
There's only the two of us left from Squad 11.
Right.
But I'm sure we'll grow again.
We have the Doctor and the commander, after all.

opts: How is the Corps faring, Clovisia?

lines.x: The retreat was initiated on-time, but we suffered heavy casualties.
And we're facing a supply shortage after Allerdale went missing.
If only we had more hands...
You've got a few here.
Totter?
My employer has gone missing, so my contract is now null and void.
Are you sure you want to stay?
We're already here. Hard to get out when you're on the wrong side of the Sarkaz anyway.
And we fought alongside Dagda's group in the mausoleum.
I may not have made it out alive, if not for them.
Another debt... I guess I need to push my retirement back.
Doctor, we're here.
Sorry. I wished we'd moved quicker and helped you at the command tower.
And you are...?
Horn, leader of the 2nd squad of the Tempest Platoon of Victoria.
Misery helped us break into a prison at the border of Auchterigg and Highbury a few hours ago.
We rescued a Defense Force patrol that was about to be executed for refusing to fight for the Sarkaz.
We'll be operating with Rhodes Island and the Self-Salvation Corps from this point on.

opts: Looks like we have some new friends, Clovisia.

lines.x: Thank you, everyone.
It's a pleasant surprise, one that has opened up many possibilities.
Although we took heavy losses, we did manage to obtain important information about the Sarkaz supply lines.
Commander, Doctor, here's the intel on potential depots in Highbury.
Add to that the data that Closure obtained from the command tower...

opts: We'll be able to locate the supply line.
The hint to our next step lies within this intel.

lines.x: Understood.
We'll make preparations for the battles ahead.
Kyngasycath, the Sighs of Kings.
I know you're there, Gawain.
Show yourself.
There is no one around her.
...Duty.
You've been with me all this time. What is the duty that you've chosen for me?
To put a king on the throne of Victoria again?
Silence.
Allerdale... she aspired to be a Steam Knight, yet...
We can't always know what we're heading towards.
If, one day—
If, one day I strayed from the destiny that you chose for me.
Would you become my nightmare?
The only sound in the underground space is a quiet sigh.
...Serious.
But Shining is here. She'll recover.
If the Banshee Lord and I arrived in time...

opts: We got away only because you slowed the 'Red-Eye'.

lines.x: She's recovering. You can see it. She won't let herself sleep for too long.

opts: I didn't see what she saw.
I'm never there with her when I should be.

lines.x: You've always cared a lot about Amiya. All the versions of you.

opts: I never had a chance to ask.
Did you suspect that I was pretending to have amnesia?

lines.x: My dagger will answer that question.
...When necessary.

opts: I want to know the truth of what happened four years ago.
But I don't know what's true and what's not.
I hardly ever get a chance to sit down and talk to you.
Can you tell me what you saw?

lines.x: Four years ago, you sent me on a mission away from the landship.
Her Majesty had left us by the time I returned.

opts: What was the situation at Rhodes Island?

lines.x: Rhodes Island had to escape before Theresis's army surrounded us.
Amiya was in a coma after inheriting the King of Sarkaz's powers.
She wanted to save Rhodes Island, save Amiya, and save you.
It was Her Majesty's final order.
Doctor... Doctor... Dr. Doctor...
Amiya grabs your clothing subconsciously, then slowly loosens her grip.
Tears flow from her eyes.
Theresa...
She showed me the voices of the Sarkaz souls.
They wanted to take revenge on everyone who has wronged the Sarkaz. They wanted to take revenge on Dr. Kal'tsit.

opts: What does this have to do with her?

lines.x: ...I believe that everything I felt was real, Doctor.
I remember how Dr. Kal'tsit and Theresa spent many nights talking at Rhodes Island.
I remember how Dr. Kal'tsit went a whole week without sleeping to save a group of Sarkaz Infected.
But it's true that she wronged the Sarkaz, two hundred years ago...
Dr. Kal'tsit must have told Theresa why she did it at the time.
And Theresa must have accepted it, which was why they became such good friends.
But even I... even I, who knows Dr. Kal'tsit so well...
I felt anger and sorrow when I felt the memories of the war.
Maybe that's what Theresa wanted to tell me.
Theresa wanted to tell me why she sided with the souls of the Sarkaz.
They are there... above Londinium.
The sound of thunder approaches.
It is outside the window, above your heads, never far away.
You hold Amiya's hand.
The two of you look up at the sky together.
Rumble. Rumble. Rumble.
Even the nearby cities feel the vibration, not just Londinium.
A massive object, the likes of which has never been seen before, rises up from the dark clouds.
Behind it, the thick clouds atop The Shard finally crack open. Those who have raised their heads to look up finally realize it was no ordinary cloud that surrounded Londinium.
It was a Catastrophe cloud, far more massive than what any Catastrophe Messenger has ever recorded.
Sparks fly out of the overcharged black clouds, rumbling like thunder.
The strange flying machine is like the eye of the storm.
Beneath its gaze, there is nowhere for the enemies of the Sarkaz to run.
2:28 P.M. / Overcast
Tradersmill City, Victoria
Chenchen!
What're you daydreamin' about? Hughes is waitin' for us. Sounds like he got a really solid lead.
The one who just left Hughes's house...
Look at this crowd. Who are you talking about?
The green-haired Feline.
I think I've seen her before.
Well, you've got a better memory for faces than I do. Maybe she was there to talk business with Hughes? Did you see her at the station a few days ago?
It's a memory from many years ago.
Not a criminal you caught in Lungmen, is she?
If that was it, I wouldn't have forgotten.
Never mind. We can just ask Hughes.
...Bagpipe.
I've seen the other in the intel too.
Hehe...
We almost had a touching reunion.
Victoria South Office
Hello.
You're... I've seen you in the sickbay at HQ, haven't I?
There's something I would like to ask of you.
Please give this letter to Dr. Kal'tsit and Dr. Doctor.
You... ahem. Are you planning to leave the office?
You should submit an application, like the other evacuating operators. Just so that HR is on top of things, you know...
They know.
They know there's something I have to do.
I'll only bring danger to the rest of you, if I stay here.
You want to protect Rhodes Island?
Rhodes Island... is a quiet place.
I like it here, and I'm thankful to you. Especially the Sankta who left us... you've given me the peace that I always sought.
And now, I repay this peace.
I'm ready.
The Sarkaz airship has taken off within Londinium.
The heir to the Duchy of Cumberland is missing. The Duke of Caster did not obtain the Sighs of Kings.
A dozen Messengers have visited your demesne in a single day.
They want to know whether you are ready to grant them an audience.
What do you think, Your Highness... Princess Eblana?
Duke Wellington...
Right before my eyes... I see a new Taran era.
